text: Figure 12.10 Pre-training for BERT-like encoder. The input tokens (and a special token denoting the start of the sequence) are converted to word embeddings. Here, these are represented as rows rather than columns, so...

In the pre-training stage, the network is trained using self-supervision. This allows the use of enormous amounts of data without the need for manual labels. For BERT, the self-supervision task consists of predicting...

Predicting missing words forces the transformer network to understand some syntax. For example, it might learn that the adjective red is often found before nouns like house or car but never before a verb like shout. I...
[^1] BERT also uses a secondary task that predicts whether two sentences were originally adjacent in the text or not, but this only marginally improves performance.
